summary:
Bilateral mandibular processes merge into one structure forming the mandible(lower jaw). This fusion occurs as part of normal developmental stages without involving other facial structures like lips directly.
The bilateral mandibular processes unite to create the mandible (lower jaw), completing their development according to established anatomical pathways; this does not involve any direct contribution from the lower lip area.
